name: Cloudflare Ingestion
description: Cloudflare Logpush ingestion inventory for the SoleDrop estate — what is arriving in the SDL, by Logpush dataset and OCSF class, with volume trend and top hosts. Use it to confirm every Cloudflare source is flowing and being parsed to OCSF.

graphs.title: Cloudflare Data Ingestion Inventory
graphs.markdown: Live inventory of Cloudflare Logpush flowing into the SDL, parsed to OCSF. The number tiles show total volume and per-dataset counts; the trend shows ingestion continuity over the window; the table is one row per **Logpush dataset x OCSF class** landing in the lake. New datasets appear automatically — nothing is hardcoded. A dataset reading **0** (or missing from the table) means nothing arrived in the window: widen the time picker before assuming an ingestion outage.

graphs.title: HTTP Requests
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset = 'HTTP Requests'
| group c=count()
| limit 1

graphs.title: Firewall Events
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset = 'Firewall events'
| group c=count()
| limit 1

graphs.title: Gateway DNS Queries
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset = 'Gateway DNS'
| group c=count()
| limit 1

graphs.title: Ingestion Volume Over Time by Dataset
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset = *
| group c=count() by dataset=dataSource.cloudflare_dataset, timestamp=timebucket('1h')
| transpose dataset on timestamp

graphs.title: Events by Dataset
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset = *
| group c=count() by dataset=dataSource.cloudflare_dataset
| filter c>0
| columns dataset, c

graphs.title: Top Hosts by Event Volume
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare' http_request.url.hostname = *
| group events=count() by host=http_request.url.hostname
| sort -events
| limit 15
| columns "Host"=host, "Events"=events

graphs.title: Ingestion Inventory (by dataset + OCSF class)
graphs.query:
dataSource.vendor = 'Cloudflare'
| group events = count(), first_ns = min(timestamp), last_ns = max(timestamp), parser = any(unmapped.parser) by vendor = dataSource.vendor, dataset = dataSource.cloudflare_dataset, ocsf_class = class_name, class_uid
| let first_seen = simpledateformat(first_ns, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z'), last_seen = simpledateformat(last_ns, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z')
| sort -events
| columns "Data Source" = vendor, "Logpush Dataset" = dataset, "OCSF Class" = ocsf_class, "Class UID" = class_uid, "Parser" = parser, "Events" = events, "First Seen" = first_seen, "Last Seen" = last_seen
